theory.canonical_statement: ID 439 establishes the final thermodynamic efficiency of the Trawin Generator. While the zero point energy may correspond to high effective temperatures, the extractable work is finite due to regularization and entropy costs. Overall efficiency for first generation devices is projected in the range of a few percent, primarily limited by mode coupling resonance and thermalization losses to the bath. Carnot Bound: The system respects the upper limit eta = 1 Tcold /Thot as a quantum heat engine. Duty Cycle Optimization: Efficiency is further gated by the ratio tauextract /taucycle.

While the zero point energy may correspond to high effective temperatures, the extractable work is finite due to regularization and entropy costs. Overall efficiency for first generation devices is projected in the range of a few percent, primarily limited by mode coupling resonance and thermalization losses to the bath. Carnot Bound: The system respects the upper limit eta = 1 Tcold /Thot as a quantum heat engine. Duty Cycle Optimization: Efficiency is further gated by the ratio tauextract /taucycle.
